title: Comparison of 1 Versus 2 Doses of Quadrivalent Influenza Vaccine in 3-8-Year-Old Children with Different Immunological States - Jiangsu Province, China, 2021.
summary: A clinical trial assessing the immunogenicity and safety of one versus two doses of the quadrivalent influenza vaccine (QIV) in children aged 3–8 years in China, stratified by previous vaccination history.
key_findings: Evidence supports a single dose of QIV for children aged 3–8 years who have previously received two or more doses of influenza vaccine.
Children who have not been previously vaccinated should still adhere to the recommended schedule of receiving two doses.
population: healthy children aged 3–8 years, Jiangsu Province, China
data_availability_statement: Supplementary data to this article can be found online.
